sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: A letter from a member to the SFA RE Barclays PLC
feed.summary: A member of the Scottish Football Supporters Association (SFSA) has written to the SFA to express deep concern over its sponsorship agreement with Barclays PLC, which includes naming Hampden Park as ‘Barclays Hampden’. The member argues that Barclays is complicit in facilitating the conflict in Gaza and illegal Israeli settlements by investing in companies supplying weapons and military technology to Israel. The letter urges the SFA to withdraw from the deal due to moral implications and potential public relations disaster, calling for solidarity with Scottish values.

feed.title: Ngai-Pesic buys Silvaco Group shares worth $103k
feed.summary: Katherine S. Ngai-Pesic, a director and 10% owner at Silvaco Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:SVCO), purchased 25,000 shares of common stock for $103,305 on December 10, 2025. This transaction increased her direct holdings to 10,303,886 shares. The acquisition follows Silvaco's reported record Q3 2025 revenue, a restructuring plan, and analyst ratings, with Rosenblatt lowering its price target to $9.00 while Needham reiterated a Buy rating at $10.00.

feed.title: Mondelēz International Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.50 per Share
feed.summary: Mondelēz International declared a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share of Class A common stock, payable on January 14, 2026, to shareholders of record by December 31, 2025. This dividend highlights the company's financial health, commitment to shareholder returns, and strong market presence with approximately $36.4 billion in net revenues for 2024. The snack food giant, known for brands like Oreo and Cadbury, also maintains its commitment to sustainability as a member of the Dow Jones Best-in-Class indices.

feed.title: Ticketmaster, Live Nation face lawsuit from concertgoers over monopoly and high ticket fees
feed.summary: Millions of Ticketmaster customers are moving towards class certification in a major antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation Entertainment and its Ticketmaster subsidiary. The lawsuit alleges that the company inflates ticket prices and uses its monopoly power to control the live events industry through exclusive contracts, excessive fees, and coercive venue practices. If certified, Live Nation-Ticketmaster could face billions in liabilities and potentially a breakup, leading to lower ticket fees and increased competition.

feed.title: Cult classic chain praised as 'better than Texas Roadhouse' to open 2 new spots
feed.summary: Olive Garden, a chain praised by some as "better than Texas Roadhouse," is expanding with two new locations: one in Greenville, Texas opening December 15th, and another in Piqua, Ohio opening January 12th, 2026. This expansion comes as parent company Darden Restaurants plans to open 65 new restaurants across its brands between May 2025 and May 2026. Additionally, Olive Garden is bringing back two discontinued menu items, Braised Beef Tortelloni and Ravioli di Portobello, until March 22, 2026.

feed.title: Pre-Earnings Spotlight: Costco's E-Commerce Engine Revs Up
feed.summary: Costco is set to report its fiscal first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating an 11.5% increase in EPS and an 8.3% rise in total revenues. The company's membership-based model, strong e-commerce growth, and focus on value-conscious offerings are expected to drive results amid a challenging retail environment. Despite a high valuation, Costco's consistent track record of exceeding earnings estimates suggests potential for renewed stock momentum.

feed.title: ServiceNow (NOW) Deepens Its Long-Term Commitment to Canada with CA$110 Million Investment; Analysts Remain Bullish
feed.summary: ServiceNow, Inc. announced a CA$110 million investment to deepen its commitment to Canada, focusing on accelerating AI adoption in the public sector and establishing a new Canada Centre of Excellence. This initiative will fund AI-ready digital infrastructure and create approximately 100 high-skilled jobs. Analysts remain bullish, with Barclays reiterating a "Buy" rating and a $1,225 price target after ServiceNow's plans to acquire identity security leader Veza.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) demos 5G network linking federal and border systems
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) successfully demonstrated a software-defined network that allows various federal agencies to communicate instantly across domains using 5G technology. The demonstration, which included tactical communications, counter-UAS capabilities, and mobile operations centers, highlighted interoperable solutions for national security and rapid response. This initiative addresses the need for advanced border security and homeland protection with efficient, rapid deployment of capabilities.

feed.title: Keysight and KT SAT Achieve Industry-First Multi-Orbit NTN Handover Between GEO Satellite and Emulated LEO Link
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies and KT SAT successfully demonstrated the industry's first multi-orbit non-terrestrial network (NTN) handover between a commercial geostationary earth orbit (GEO) satellite and an emulated low Earth orbit (LEO) link. This achievement, performed over a live Ku-band GEO connection, validates a core 6G capability and marks a significant step towards continuous multi-orbit mobility for resilient, always-on coverage in remote or disaster-affected areas. The collaboration showcases how laboratory emulation can accelerate the development and deployment of next-generation satellite communication services.

feed.title: Maple links with OpenTable (BKNG) to power 24/7 AI phone reservations for restaurants
feed.summary: Maple, a leading Voice AI platform for restaurants, has announced a strategic integration with OpenTable, a global leader in restaurant technology. This partnership enables restaurants to automate customer calls, enhance operational efficiency, and seamlessly manage reservations using AI-powered voice technology around the clock. The integration aims to reduce missed calls, convert more inquiries into confirmed reservations, and free up staff to focus on in-person hospitality.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls Industries stock hits all-time high at 330.0 USD
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) stock has reached a new all-time high of $330.00 USD, reflecting a 72.55% increase over the past year. This growth is attributed to strategic initiatives and strong performance in the defense and shipbuilding industries. Recent company highlights include progress on the ROMULUS unmanned surface vessel, a keel laying ceremony for the Virginia-class submarine Barb, deployment of the GRIMM spectrum dominance system, and a significant contract from the U.S. Navy for aircraft carrier engineering support.

feed.title: Why Chubb Stock Just Popped
feed.summary: Chubb Limited (NYSE: CB) shares increased by 3.6% following reports of an informal takeover approach to American International Group (NYSE: AIG). Although both companies deny a formal offer, the speculation led to a stock jump, possibly due to investors believing Chubb will not acquire AIG and will continue its strong independent performance as an undervalued stock with a modest dividend.

feed.title: DoorDash, Uber Sue NYC Over Mandate to Show Tips Prompt Upfront
feed.summary: DoorDash and Uber Technologies Inc. have jointly sued New York City to challenge new laws requiring tipping options to be presented at checkout with a minimum of 10%. The companies argue this could negatively impact consumers already facing inflation. These laws, set to take effect in late January, emerged after the city's minimum-wage rule for delivery workers led companies to adjust service fees and shift tipping functions to post-checkout.

feed.title: Guggenheim Initiates Coverage of Amazon.com (AMZN) with "Buy" Rating and $300 Price Target
feed.summary: Guggenheim has initiated coverage of Amazon.com (AMZN) with a "Buy" rating and a $300 price target, acknowledging the retail sector's resilience despite perceived weaknesses and strong gross margins. Rosenblatt Securities also reiterated a "Buy" rating with a $305 target, citing strong enterprise adoption, Amazon's growing role in AI and cloud markets, and undervalued cash flow potential. The company's recent launch of faster and more energy-efficient Graviton5-based EC2 M9g instances further highlights its technological advancements.

feed.title: Intuit (INTU) Sees Positive Analyst Sentiment Following Strong Fiscal Q1 2026 Results
feed.summary: Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ: INTU) is experiencing positive analyst sentiment following its strong fiscal Q1 2026 results, driven by an expanding AI-expert platform and broad-based momentum across its segments. The company reported an 18% YoY revenue increase to $3.9 billion, with significant growth in Global Business Solutions, Online Ecosystem, and QuickBooks Online Account, alongside reaffirming its full-year fiscal 2026 guidance.

feed.title: Bullish Analyst Sentiment on Salesforce (CRM) Amid Expanding Enterprise Demand and Strong Q3 Results
feed.summary: Analysts express bullish sentiment on Salesforce (CRM) following strong Q3 2026 results and expanding enterprise demand for its AI-driven solutions. The company reported a 9% YoY revenue increase and significant growth in operating and free cash flow, driven by its Agentforce and Data Cloud portfolios. RBC Capital reiterated its "Sector Perform" rating, while CMB International's Saiyi He maintained a "Buy" rating, citing the company's product breadth and financial trajectory.

feed.title: NSC Stock Update: Deutsche Bank Downgrades Norfolk Southern | NS
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has downgraded Norfolk Southern (NSC) from "Buy" to "Hold," significantly reducing its price target from $354.00 to $297.00, reflecting a 16.10% decrease. This cautionary move by Deutsche Bank, led by analyst Richa Harnain, marks a notable shift in sentiment compared to other analysts who recently maintained or slightly adjusted their ratings. Despite the downgrade, other analysts' forecasts suggest an average target price of $310.80 for NSC, implying a 5.66% upside from the current price.

feed.title: 9 ESG benefits for businesses
feed.summary: ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) frameworks are gaining widespread popularity in the business world, driven by increasing investor and stakeholder interest in sustainability and ethical practices. Implementing a solid ESG strategy offers numerous benefits, including attracting investors, building customer loyalty, improving financial performance, gaining a competitive advantage, and making operations more sustainable. These benefits apply to businesses of all sizes, contributing to long-term value creation and resilience in a changing market.

feed.title: eBay Chief Privacy Officer, VP AI & Data Responsibility Anna Zeiter Departs
feed.summary: Anna Zeiter, eBay's Chief Privacy Officer and VP AI & Data Responsibility for over 11 years, has announced her departure, raising questions about the future of the company's responsible AI and privacy initiatives. Her exit comes amidst increased regulatory scrutiny in Europe over eBay's use of user data for AI training and concerns from sellers regarding AI altering listing information without explicit permission. The article explores the potential legal ramifications for eBay's liability protections under Section 230 given its AI practices.

feed.title: Is Colgate Palmolive Offering Value as DCF Signals 36.7% Upside in 2025?
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) is explored for its investment value, with shares at $77.76 and modest returns over the past 3-5 years. While a Discounted Cash Flow model suggests a 36.7% undervaluation, implying a target price of $122.83, its Price-to-Earnings ratio of 21.55x is higher than the industry average, signaling it may be modestly overvalued by that metric. The article encourages investors to consider different "Narratives" to form their own valuation based on future assumptions.

feed.title: U.S. Approves NVIDIA’s (NVDA) H200 Processor Exports to China, CEO Flags China’s AI Infrastructure Advantage
feed.summary: The U.S. has approved NVIDIA Corporation's exports of H200 processors to China, subject to a 25% fee, while still restricting its flagship Blackwell chips. This decision is seen as a strategic compromise to balance national security with economic interests, preventing a full ban that could boost Huawei within China. Despite this, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has warned about China's growing AI infrastructure advantage, urging the U.S. to address this disparity urgently.

feed.title: AETH Entertainment and Kartoon Studios Partner To Launch TaleSphere Studios; Will Focus On Live-Action With ‘Real Santa’ As Debut Film
feed.summary: Kartoon Studios and AETH Entertainment have formed TaleSphere Studios, a new joint venture focusing on live-action youth and family entertainment. The partnership aims to capitalize on the growing success of the family film genre, with their first project being "Real Santa," based on the novel by William Hazelgrove. This venture marks Kartoon Studios' push into live-action content, aligning with AETH Entertainment's expertise in profitably producing entertainment.

feed.title: Campbell Molly sells East West Bancorp (EWBC) stock worth $105,400
feed.summary: Campbell Molly, a Director at East West Bancorp (NASDAQ:EWBC), sold 1,000 shares of company stock for a total of $105,400. This transaction occurred as the bank reported strong third-quarter earnings, surpassing market expectations, and maintained a "GREAT" financial health score according to InvestingPro. Keefe, Bruyette & Woods reiterated an Outperform rating with a $124 price target due to solid performance.

feed.title: 3E and ServiceNow Announce Partnership to Provide Chemical Management Solution
feed.summary: 3E, a leader in chemical and regulatory data solutions, has partnered with ServiceNow to power its new Health and Safety Chemical Management Solution. 3E will provide Safety Data Sheets (SDS) and indexed chemical data, forming the foundation of ServiceNow's chemical management framework. This collaboration aims to simplify chemical compliance, enhance regulatory adherence, and reduce risk for ServiceNow customers by integrating 3E's content directly into ServiceNow’s AI Platform.

feed.title: Fed Cuts Rates, Signals Caution Ahead: 5 Bank Stocks Set to Benefit
feed.summary: The Federal Reserve announced its third interest rate cut of 25 basis points, lowering the Fed funds rates to 3.5%-3.75%, amid persistent inflation and a softening job market, impacting rate-sensitive financial services. This decision is expected to benefit several bank stocks, including Citigroup, Bank of America, KeyCorp, Wells Fargo, and Citizens Financial, through support for net interest income expansion, reduced default risks, and increased lending activity. The Fed's dot plot signals further rate cuts in 2026 and 2027, with economic growth anticipated to rise and inflation and unemployment rates to fall.

feed.title: How UCaaS solves the cost of poor communication
feed.summary: Businesses often suffer from poor communication due to fragmented tools, lack of integration, and reliance on legacy systems, leading to reduced productivity and financial drain. Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS) offers a strategic solution by providing an integrated, cloud-based platform that addresses these issues, fostering better collaboration and offering clear ROI benefits. Migrating to UCaaS helps businesses modernize communication, streamline operations, and support hybrid work models.

feed.title: KMX INVESTOR ALERT: CarMax (KMX) Securities Fraud Lawsuit Filed Over Alleged Concealed Demand Pull-Forward and Auto Finance Portfolio Risk – Hagens Berman
feed.summary: Hagens Berman has filed a securities fraud lawsuit against CarMax (KMX) alleging that the company misled investors about the stability of its business by concealing a temporary "pull-forward of customer demand" and escalating risks in its auto finance portfolio. The lawsuit highlights two major stock drops totaling 44%, following a dismal Q2 earnings report and the unexpected termination of the CEO. Investors who suffered significant losses during the Class Period (June 20, 2025, to November 5, 2025) are urged to contact Hagens Berman before the January 2, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share for $870
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC recently purchased a single share of Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL) for $870, increasing its direct ownership to 1,162,583 shares. This transaction follows Texas Pacific Land Corporation's Q3 2025 earnings which missed analyst expectations, as well as the announcement of a three-for-one stock split effective December 22, 2025. KeyBanc has also initiated coverage on Texas Pacific Land Trust with an Overweight rating and a $1,050 price target.

feed.title: Dollar General Grows Executive Roster Amid FY2026 Expansion Plans
feed.summary: Dollar General has announced strategic leadership changes to support its significant expansion plans for fiscal year 2026. The company promoted Matthew Simonsen to SVP of business intelligence and development, April Crofford to VP of store facilities management, and Tito Rydinsky to division VP of store operations. These adjustments follow a strong Q3 earnings report and aim to bolster Dollar General's efforts to add 450 new locations, remodel 2,000 stores, and open 10 new stores in Mexico.

feed.title: What does the Netflix-Warner deal mean for talent?
feed.summary: Netflix's $82.7 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. is raising significant concerns within the entertainment industry, particularly regarding fair pay for actors and writers. The deal, which further consolidates streaming platforms, has prompted SAG-AFTRA and the Directors Guild of America (DGA) to express worries about its impact on creative talent and the competitive landscape. This acquisition reignites long-standing issues surrounding residuals and upfront pay in the streaming era.

feed.title: Why Is XOMA Royalty Stock Sinking Thursday?
feed.summary: XOMA Royalty Corporation (NASDAQ: XOMA) stock plunged after its partner Rezolute Inc. (NASDAQ: RZLT) announced that its Phase 3 sunRIZE study for ersodetug (RZ358), a treatment for congenital hyperinsulinism, did not meet its primary endpoint. The study, evaluating ersodetug in patients with congenital hyperinsulinism, failed to show a statistically significant reduction in hypoglycemia events compared to placebo. This setback for the partnered product led to significant drops in both XOMA Royalty and Rezolute shares.

feed.title: Exxon Is Slashing Low-Carbon Spending — And Quietly Betting On A Fossil-Fuel Supercycle
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil's updated 2030 Plan shows a clear shift, with less investment in low-carbon initiatives and increased focus on current fossil fuel assets. The company projects significant earnings and cash flow growth by 2030, primarily from its upstream oil and gas operations like the Permian and Guyana. This strategy suggests a belief that fossil fuel cash flows will remain strong for an extended period, positioning Exxon as a leader in a trend back towards traditional energy fundamentals.

feed.title: Is the Market Bullish or Bearish on Moderna Inc?
feed.summary: Moderna Inc.'s short interest as a percent of float has increased by 5.79% since its last report, with 68.08 million shares sold short, representing 21.18% of available regular shares. This increase suggests a more bearish market sentiment towards the stock. When compared to its peers, Moderna has a higher short interest percentage, indicating that more investors are betting against the company's stock.

feed.title: January 2026 Options Now Available For Intuitive Surgical (ISRG)
feed.summary: New options for Intuitive Surgical Inc (ISRG) for the January 2026 expiration began trading today. BNK Invest highlighted a $545.00 strike put option and a $555.00 strike call option, detailing potential returns and risks for investors considering these contracts based on the current stock price of $549.18/share.

feed.title: Global Payments Sinks 28.3% YTD: Dip Worth Buying or Just Dead Weight?
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (GPN) has seen a significant 28.3% decline year-to-date, prompting questions about its investment potential. The impending acquisition of Worldpay and divestiture of GPN’s Issuer Solutions unit are expected to transform it into a pure-play merchant solutions company, which analysts believe will enhance its operational focus and scale. Despite competitive pressures and elevated expenses, the company's strong financial health and discounted valuation suggest potential upside for long-term investors.

feed.title: A Closer Look at Arista Networks's Options Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Large investors have shown a bullish sentiment towards Arista Networks (NYSE: ANET) through recent options trades, with a significant majority of detected trades being calls. The projected price range for ANET is between $100.0 and $240.0 for the next three months. Currently, the stock is up by 2.98% at $136.31, with analysts maintaining a "Neutral" rating and an average price target of $140.0.

feed.title: Citi Upgrades Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to Buy, Raises Price Target
feed.summary: Citi has upgraded Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to a "buy" rating and raised its price target to $660, anticipating increased R&D spending by pharmaceutical companies will benefit the firm. Despite its strong financial health and market position in medical diagnostics and research, investors should monitor sector-specific challenges and insider selling activity. The company shows robust profitability and a healthy balance sheet, though gross margins have seen a long-term decline.

feed.title: Kroger to build $391 million distribution center in Kentucky, creating 430 jobs
feed.summary: Kroger, the largest traditional grocer in the nation, plans to invest $391 million into a new distribution center in Simpson County, Kentucky. This facility is expected to create 430 new full-time jobs, significantly boosting the state's distribution and logistics sector. This new investment follows Kroger's recent $204 million expansion in Bowling Green.

feed.title: Costco has favorable set up going into Q1 earnings report, analysts say
feed.summary: Analysts at UBS have reiterated a 'Buy' rating for Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST, XETRA:CTO) ahead of its fiscal first quarter earnings report. They anticipate the retailer will maintain strong momentum through the holiday season, driven by consistent sales growth, market share gains, and expanding operating margins. UBS projects net sales growth of 8.2% and core comparable sales increase of 6.4% for Q1 2026, slightly above consensus, and an EPS of $4.39.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider VeriSign After Its Recent 18% Year To Date Surge
feed.summary: VeriSign (VRSN) has surged 18.1% year to date and 22.2% over the last year, reflecting strong momentum and investor refocus on its core internet infrastructure role and contract-based revenue. Despite recent minor dips, the stock is considered undervalued by Simply Wall St's valuation framework, particularly its discounted cash flow (DCF) analysis, which prices it very close to its calculated fair value of $243.75 per share compared to the current $242. Its P/E ratio of 27.7x is also below the broader IT industry average, suggesting a relative discount despite solid fundamentals.

feed.title: Barclays Has Lowered Expectations for Lennox International (NYSE:LII) Stock Price
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for Lennox International (NYSE:LII) from $700 to $680, though it maintained an "overweight" rating. Several other analysts have also cut their targets, leading to a MarketBeat consensus rating of "Hold" with an average price target near $596.83. This adjustment comes after Lennox's recent quarter beat EPS expectations but saw a slight revenue decline and lowered FY2025 EPS guidance.

feed.title: Globe Life (NYSE:GL) Stock Price Expected to Rise, TD Cowen Analyst Says
feed.summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Globe Life (NYSE:GL) from $182.00 to $199.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and suggesting a substantial 43.45% upside. This comes as Globe Life reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates with $4.81 EPS against an expected $4.54. The company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus from analysts, with an average target of $165.00.

feed.title: Ur-Energy Inc. Stock Dips Amid Intensifying Market Dynamics
feed.summary: Ur-Energy Inc.'s stock has dipped by 13.6% due to heightened market volatility and negative profitability metrics, despite a significant revenue rise over three years. The company faces immediate cash flow concerns but is strategically diversifying into new energy solutions. Market reactions have been cautious, with investors awaiting the impact of Ur-Energy's transformative steps and strategic realignments.

feed.title: Why Kraft Heinz’s Suppliers Hold the Key to Net Zero
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz's path to net zero by 2050 heavily relies on its diverse supplier network, as approximately 80% of its carbon footprint stems from these relationships. The company is focusing on transforming farming practices for key ingredients like tomatoes and enhancing packaging sustainability to achieve its ambitious environmental goals. Through initiatives like sustainable tomato sourcing, renewable energy adoption in manufacturing, and reducing virgin plastic use, Kraft Heinz aims to drive a culture of sustainability across its entire supply chain.

feed.title: Steven Madden (NASDAQ:SHOO) Hits New 12-Month High - Here's Why
feed.summary: Steven Madden (NASDAQ:SHOO) recently hit a new 52-week high, trading at $44.96, spurred by favorable analyst ratings including a target price increase to $50 from Piper Sandler. Despite slightly missing Q3 earnings and revenue expectations, the company provided Q4 2025 EPS guidance and announced a quarterly dividend. Institutional investors hold a significant portion of the stock, reflecting confidence in the company's performance.

feed.title: The latest from the evolving AI channel: 11 December: ThoughtSpot, F5, NetApp...
feed.summary: This article details several recent AI technology announcements relevant to the channel from December 11, 2025. It covers new offerings from companies like ThoughtSpot, Precisely, F5 (in collaboration with NetApp), Checkmarx (acquiring Tromzo), EY (with NVIDIA), Coralogix, Orgvue, and Panaseer, all focusing on advancements in AI agents, security, data management, and operational efficiency across various industries.

feed.title: Texas AT&T customers still have time to claim up to $7,500 in breach settlement
feed.summary: Texas AT&T customers have until December 18th to claim their share of a $177 million settlement stemming from two data breaches in 2024. Customers affected by the first incident (AT&T 1) could receive up to $5,000, while those impacted by the second incident (AT&T 2) could claim up to $2,000. Individuals affected by both breaches can file separate claims, potentially receiving a total of $7,500.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment director Don R. Kornstein to resign at year-end By Investing.com
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment, Inc. (NASDAQ:CZR) announced that director Don R. Kornstein will resign from its Board of Directors, effective December 31, 2025. The company stated this is not due to any disagreement, and no replacement information has been provided. This news comes amidst recent shifts in analyst ratings for CZR, with Wells Fargo initiating coverage at Equal Weight, Jefferies downgrading to Hold, and CFRA solidifying a Sell rating due to debt concerns.

feed.title: Timberland Bancorp appoints Kevin Sakamoto as chief credit officer By Investing.com
feed.summary: Timberland Bancorp (NASDAQ: TSBK) has announced the appointment of Kevin Sakamoto as its new chief credit officer, effective Thursday. This follows the mutual agreement to cancel the employment of Todd Van Cise, who had served in the role since January 2024. Sakamoto, 57, brings over 30 years of commercial banking experience to the position, having previously served as vice president of credit administration and special assets for the company since 2023.

feed.title: AMD (AMD) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
feed.summary: Shares of AMD fell 3.8% due to disappointing earnings from Oracle, raising concerns about enterprise AI spending, and a US policy shift benefiting Nvidia in the China market. This has led to fears that companies with tightening budgets may opt for Nvidia over AMD. Despite the drop, AMD is up 77.8% year-to-date, though it's still below its 52-week high.
